title: Snowflake Semantic View Import, Export, and Scheduled Sync
story: As a data analyst, I want to import Snowflake semantic views and models into DataTools Pro and export updated YML back to Snowflake, so that I can manage and synchronize my semantic layer definitions alongside my metrics glossary.
description:
Enable bidirectional sync between DataTools Pro and Snowflake semantic views/models through Metrics Analyst.

### Import
- Import Snowflake semantic views and semantic models via Metrics Analyst
- Semantic views are treated as reports in the analytics hierarchy (dashboards → reports → models)
- Import must parse the full semantic view but focus only on measures and metrics
- Fetch metrics, facts, and dimensions and store them in dedicated models

### Export
- Export updated YML to update Snowflake semantic views and models
- YML includes tables, facts, dimensions, and metrics with comments, expressions, and synonyms
- Export generates `CREATE OR REPLACE SEMANTIC VIEW` SQL statements

### Metric Reconciliation
- When importing, match proposed metrics against existing glossary using `similar_text()` string matching
- Match options: Assign semantic view name to existing metric, Create as alias, Keep both (create new with 'reviewed' status)
- 100% match suppresses 'Create as alias' option
- Proposed metric expressions are stored as logic definitions when added

### Sync
- Refresh semantic view data with last-refresh timestamp
- Scheduled sync to continuously monitor for changes

### Architecture Considerations
- Executing `CREATE OR REPLACE` SQL in customer Snowflake instances raises permission concerns
- Potential solution: Snowflake native app extension that connects to DataTools Pro via REST API
- Build middleware functions in DataTools Pro first for testing, then consider porting to native app
acceptance_criteria: Semantic views can be imported with their metrics, facts, and dimensions
Imported metrics are matched against existing glossary using string similarity scoring
User can assign semantic view name, create alias, or keep both when matches are found
YML export generates valid CREATE OR REPLACE SEMANTIC VIEW SQL
Refresh updates semantic view data and stamps last-refresh date
100% match suppresses the Create as alias option
Metric expressions from semantic views are stored as logic definitions
ux_changes: Semantic view import wizard with metric reconciliation UI showing match scores and action options (assign, alias, keep both). View button for inspecting existing metrics during reconciliation. Refresh button with last-refresh date display.
middleware_changes: Semantic view parser for extracting metrics, facts, and dimensions. String similarity matching using similar_text() for metric reconciliation. YML/SQL generator for semantic view export. Refresh logic with timestamp tracking.
data_model_changes: Dedicated models for semantic view metrics, facts, and dimensions. Logic definitions linked to metrics for Snowflake expressions. Last-refresh timestamp on semantic views.
api_changes: Snowflake SQL statement REST API integration for semantic view operations. Potential future REST API in Snowflake native app for bidirectional sync.
implementation_notes: Current YML export format includes tables with base_table references, primary keys, facts (name/expr/data_type), and metrics (name/description/expr/synonyms). CREATE OR REPLACE SEMANTIC VIEW SQL generated with full table/fact/dimension/metric definitions and CA extension JSON. Similar_text() matching uses strtolower() for case-insensitive comparison against metric names and all alias names. View button implemented for inspecting existing metrics during reconciliation. Ryan suggested building middleware in DataTools Pro first, then potentially porting to a Snowflake native app for better permission handling.
comment_summary: Waqar shared the current YML export format and asked about creating semantic views from it. Implementation progressed through semantic view import (metrics/facts/dimensions), metric reconciliation with match scoring and 4 action options, view button for existing metrics, and refresh with timestamp. Discussion about executing SQL in customer Snowflake instances led to considering a native app bridge. Ryan approved building middleware in DataTools Pro first for testing.
